what_it_does: Records a standing correction that the current forms taxonomy and repo_digest kind enum in the-one-application are provisional AI scaffolding, not Tim's intended taxonomy, and directs that a canonical taxonomy should be derived from open-ended codebase coverage rather than imposed in advance.
observations: The file is explicitly a placeholder warning, dated 2026-06-17 and attributed to Tim.
It lists the current forms/ rows (decision, log, registry, prose) and repo_digest's kind enum (code, doc, config, test, data) as prior-AI scaffolding.
It states the underlying mechanism (file-discovered form registry, effort-routing-by-form, lifters for free structure) may be sound even though the specific taxonomy is provisional.
It cites a principle that forms should come from coverage outputs, not be forced onto coverage.
It references [[feedback-scaffolding-not-spec]], [[the-one-application]], and the coverage runbook.
standouts: Labels the existing forms taxonomy as 'PRIOR-AI SCAFFOLDING' and warns not to build on it as canonical.
Directs an open-ended, describe-what-is-there coverage pass before proposing a real taxonomy.
Explicitly names the placeholder artifacts: forms/ rows and repo_digest kind enum.
conventions: Markdown document with a title, attribution line, quoted speaker block, and explanatory sections.
Uses bold emphasis for normative statements and wiki-style [[...]] links for related concepts.
Self-identifies as a correction/placeholder rather than a specification.
Includes a date and named author in the header.
concerns: The file leaves the canonical taxonomy undefined and relies on a future, unspecified redesign process.
It warns against using existing artifacts but does not provide an interim replacement taxonomy.
notes: A meta-warning that the current forms taxonomy is provisional scaffolding and must be redesigned from coverage evidence, not used as-is.
questions: What are Tim's specific principles and architecture that will shape the canonical taxonomy?
What is the exact procedure and acceptance criteria in the coverage runbook?
How will the transition from placeholder forms to canonical forms be tracked and communicated?
What parts of the base mechanism are considered sound and which are at risk?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — it presents itself as a standing correction about placeholder status, and the content does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Authorial ownership of design (Tim vs prior AI output).
Separation of mechanism from specific taxonomy.
intention_signals: To stop downstream work from treating an AI-generated provisional forms taxonomy as final and to reroute taxonomy design through open-ended codebase coverage aligned with Tim's principles.
novelty: It is unusual as a file whose main role is to declare that adjacent placeholder taxonomies should not be treated as final and to prescribe a coverage-first derivation path.
contribution: It preserves the recorded caveat that the current forms taxonomy is provisional and the directive that a taxonomy should be derived from open-ended coverage evidence rather than inherited from scaffolding.
summary_for_embedding: A placeholder correction file stating that the current forms taxonomy and repo_digest kind enum in the-one-application are provisional AI scaffolding, not canonical, and that the real taxonomy should be derived from open-ended codebase coverage.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent hardcoding of a provisional AI-generated taxonomy and redirect form design to be evidence-based and coverage-driven.
